identificationModule.briefTitle: Intra-abdominal Pressure and Insufflator Effects in Robotic Surgery
identificationModule.officialTitle: Comparison of the Perioperative Effects of Intra-abdominal Pressure Created With Standard and Valveless Insufflators in Robotic Surgery

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The main aim of this study is to compare the perioperative effects of different intra-abdominal pressures and different insufflators in patients undergoing robotic surgery at a 30-45 degree trendelenburg position.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: Adult patients scheduled for robotic genitourinary or colorectal surgery are going to be enrolled in this study. Patients will be randomized in three groups. In group I, conventional insufflators will be used with 12 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure; in group II, valveless insufflators will be used with 12 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure and in group III, valveless insufflators will be used with 8 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure. Intraoperative data regarding airway pressures, lung compliance, hemodynamic parameters, arterial blood gas analysis, times of pressure loss and camera cleaning will be gathered. Each patient will receive a standardized anesthesia and analgesia procedure with a intravenous morphine patient controlled analgesia (PCA). Postoperative pain will be monitored at 1st, 3rd, 6th, 12th and 24th postoperative hours and morphine consumption rates will be recorded. Patient length of stay, time to first flatus, urine output, postoperative complications(graded by Clavien Dindo classification), preoperative and postoperative hemoglobin and creatinine levels will be filed.

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: The patient and the pain nurses will be blind to the intervention due to the study design.

armGroups.label: Valveless 12
armGroups.description: Patients scheduled for genitourinary or colorectal robotic surgery, valveless insufflators will be used with 12 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure.
interventionNames: Device: Valveless Insufflator with 12 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure

armGroups.label: Valveless 8
armGroups.description: Patients scheduled for genitourinary or colorectal robotic surgery, valveless insufflators will be used with 8 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure.
interventionNames: Device: Valveless Insufflator with 8 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure

interventions.name: Valveless Insufflator with 12 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure
interventions.description: In this group valveless insufflators, which are relatively new in clinical practice, will be used under low intra-abdominal pressure throughout the surgery.

interventions.name: Valveless Insufflator with 8 mmHg intra-abdominal pressure
interventions.description: In this group valveless insufflators, which are relatively new in clinical practice, will be used under ultra-low intra-abdominal pressure throughout the surgery.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Number of manipulations done by the attending anesthesiologists
primaryOutcomes.description: After the induction of general anesthesia, orotracheal intubation and arterial catheterization; a standard ventilation and anesthesia maintenance strategy will be applied. Every manipulation performed to keep the parameters monitored by the anesthesiologists within physiological limits will be recorded: ventilation frequency setting, remifentail infusion titration, administration of vasoactive agents, etc.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Arterial blood gas will be sampled at standardized times during the operation and lactate levels will be compared among the groups
Peak airway pressures (cmH2O) will be compared among the groups
Mean airway pressures (cmH2O) will be compared among the groups
Lung compliance (mL/cmH2O) will be compared among the groups
End tidal carbon dioxide levels (mmHg) will be compared among the groups
Minute ventilation (L/minute) will be compared among the groups
Systolic arterial pressures (mmHg) will be compared among the groups.
Diastolic arterial pressures (mmHg) will be compared among the groups.
Mean arterial pressures (mmHg) will be compared among the groups.
Heart rate (beat per minute) will be compared among the groups
Acute postoperative pain reported with numeric rating scale (0-10) will be recorded at postoperative 1st, 3rd, 6th, 12th and 24th hours with 0 meaning no pain and 10 meaning worst imaginable pain.
Intravenous morphine consumption (mg) will be recorded at postoperative 1st, 3rd, 6th, 12th and 24th hours
Anesthesia and operative times wil be recorded as minutes
Total bleeding level estimated by the surgical nurse will be recorded as milliliters at the end of the surgery
Kidney function will be monitored by using intraoperative and daily postoperative urine output (mL)
Kidney function will be monitored by using daily creatinine levels (mg/dL)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Preoperative and postoperative (up to one month)

secondaryOutcomes.description: Intraoperative and daily postoperative hemoglobin levels will be recorded
Blood product transfusions will be recorded

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Number of participants with pulmonary complications
secondaryOutcomes.description: Pulmonary complications like atelectasis, pulmonary oedema, pleural effusion, pneumothorax, pneumonia, ventilatory failure will be recorded for each patient. And number of patients with pulmonary complications will be compared among the groups.
Postoperative time to first flatus will be recorded
Postoperative time to oral intake will be recorded

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Number of participants with subcutaneous emphysema
secondaryOutcomes.description: Subcutaneous emphysema diagnosed with chest radiograph will be recorded
Length of hospital stay will be recorded as hours

secondaryOutcomes.measure: The Clavien-Dindo classification of surgical complications
secondaryOutcomes.description: Any postoperative complication will be graded by the Clavien-Dindo classification between I to V with I meaning any deviation from the postoperative course without the need for pharmacological treatment or surgical, endoscopic, and radiological interventions and V meaning death of a patient.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Elective robotic surgery with intraabdominal insufflation and trendelenburg position (prostatectomy, hemicolectomy etc.)
* ASA (American Society of Anesthesiologists) Physical Status I-II-III

Exclusion Criteria:

* Patients without consent
* Emergency surgery
* Bleeding diathesis
* Pregnancy or lactation
* Prior history of major abdominal/pelvic surgery
* Chronic kidney disease
* Chronic opioid consumption for chronic pain
* Inability to communicate with the patient due to language barriers or mental status

ipdSharingStatementModule.description: Data collected for the study, including de-identified individual participant data will be made available to other within 6 months after the publication of this trial, as will additional related documents (study protocol, statistical analysis plan and informed consent form), for academic purposes (e.g , meta-analyses) upon request to the corresponding author, and with a signed data access agreement.
